feed.title: HyperLightTech Blog
feed.subtitle: Production notes for builders — Linux hardening, DeFi safety, and AI agents.

entry.title: Give your AI agent a memory
entry.summary: The memory ladder in runnable Python: in-context history, a JSON file that persists between runs, and a save_memory tool so the agent decides what's worth keeping.

entry.title: Harden SSH on Ubuntu in three moves
entry.summary: If SSH is open, it's being probed right now. Ed25519 keys, a hardened sshd_config, and fail2ban — the three changes that make brute-forcing pointless, with the exact commands.

entry.title: Before you sign: verify any DeFi contract on Etherscan
entry.summary: Every on-chain transaction is public — if you know how to read it. Decode the transaction fields, then run the five-step contract check before you interact with anything new.

entry.title: The agent loop: 40 lines of Python behind every AI agent
entry.summary: Perceive, reason, act, observe. The complete loop that drives every AI agent — in runnable Python with the Claude API — plus the five design decisions that keep it from melting down.

entry.title: The five attacks that actually drain DeFi wallets
entry.summary: There are no chargebacks on-chain. Phishing, malicious approvals, seed theft, rug pulls, fake airdrops — how each one works, and the 60-second checklist that blocks them all.

entry.title: Encrypt your DNS on Ubuntu in five minutes
entry.summary: Every site you visit leaks in plain text before HTTPS even starts. Turn on DNS-over-TLS with the resolver Ubuntu already ships — the exact config, the verify steps, and the leak test.
